The government is preparing contingency plans, seeking IMF relief on petroleum levies, and exploring alternate fuel routes. This comes as fuel prices in Pakistan have already risen by approximately 20%, intensifying economic strain and concerns about further inflation.

title: Jwala delivers 5 cubs at Kuno National Park; India’s cheetah population rises to 53
description: Project Cheetah celebrates a significant milestone as Kuno National Park welcomes five new cheetah cubs, born to Namibian cheetah Jwala. This brings India's cheetah population to 53, with 33 cubs now born on Indian soil. The births signify successful adaptation and a major boost to re-establishing the species, declared extinct in 1952.

While Israel stated the depots supplied the regime, Washington conveyed strong disagreement, with concerns about rising gas prices.

description: The Narcotics Control Bureau has busted a pan-India darknet drug network, "Team Kalki," seizing significant quantities of LSD, MDMA, and liquid MDMA. Investigations are ongoing to uncover the full extent of this online trafficking ring.

title: Dehradun MCA student couple stab each other during fight over alleged affair
description: A young couple, first-year MCA students, were injured after allegedly stabbing each other in Dehradun. The altercation reportedly stemmed from accusations of infidelity, with both individuals attacking each other with kitchen knives. They are currently hospitalized and out of danger.

title: Amazon sends letter to FCC - Here's what it read
description: Amazon's Leo has urged the FCC to reject SpaceX's ambitious one million-satellite proposal, citing a lack of critical orbital and RF data. Amazon argues the plan is speculative, lacks space safety details, and would take centuries to deploy, potentially monopolizing orbital resources. The filing questions the feasibility and intent behind SpaceX's massive constellation.

title: Nashik bribery case: 2 GST officials among 3 held by Mumbai ACB over Rs 1 crore demand
description: Mumbai ACB arrested a private individual and detained two senior Maharashtra GST officials for allegedly demanding Rs 1.25 crore in bribe from a Nashik businessman. The private person was caught accepting Rs 20 lakh, the first installment, on behalf of the officials to expedite a GST investigation.
